pattern: With tiny constraints, brute-force all subarrays checking the cumulative OR; once any element alone meets the threshold the answer is 1.
hints: OR of a subarray is monotonically non-decreasing as you extend it; check all subarrays starting from each left endpoint and stop extending once the OR meets k.
The constraints are very small (n, values, k all at most 50–63) so a nested-loop O(n^2) scan easily fits in time.
If any single element is already >= k the answer is 1; otherwise track the minimum length over all qualifying subarrays, returning -1 if none found.

edge_inputs.description: single element less than k, no subarray works
need to OR all elements to reach k
all zeros with k=0, answer is 1
max element value=50, k=63 requires bitwise combination
no combination can reach k since max OR of all elements < k

stress.description: random small arrays to verify brute vs optimal agree
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 63)
    return [nums, k]
